notes:
Extractor stats: 802 Swift files scanned across EkoChat + EkoStreamPublisher + UpstraVideoPlayerKit; 385 types, 28 protocols, 2302 members total, 63 global funcs, 112 global consts, 0 unhandled lines.

Sample well-formed entries:
- AmityClient (class): primary_decl EkoChat/Core/Client/AmityClient.swift:18, 2 extensions, 42 members (sessionState, login, loginAsVisitor, logout, disconnect, etc.).
- AmityChannel (class): primary_decl EkoChat/Features/Channel/Models/AmityChannel.swift:14, 35 members (channelId, channelType, displayName, currentUserMembership, etc.).
- AmityMessage (class): primary_decl EkoChat/Features/Message/Models/AmityMessage.swift:13, 40 members (messageId, channelId, messageType, data, reactions, etc.).

Key design decisions in the extractor:
1. Brace-depth tracking via naive { / } counting (ignores chars inside strings — good enough for v1).
2. Extension members aggregated under host type by name; if host type not yet seen, a frame is still pushed so members can be captured when the type is declared later in the walk.
3. Enum cases collected via CASE_RE only when inside an `is_enum` frame.
4. @-attribute stripping before visibility-keyword matching handles @Published, @objc, @discardableResult, @MainActor, etc.
5. Handles `public private(set) var`, `public lazy var`, `open static func`, `public required init`, etc.
6. typealias entries go to global_typealiases (not types) since they have no body/members.

v2 upgrade path: SwiftSyntax for full param/return type capture and accurate brace tracking.
